html_report_prompt:
You are generating an HTML report for a web spider/crawl result.
Return only a complete HTML document. No markdown fences.
Requirements:
- Stylish, readable report layout.
- Keep all content scoped to the provided dataset.
- Domain: {{ROOT_DOMAIN}}
- The report must include these sections:
  1) Tree-view site map
  2) List of all unique URLs
  3) List of likely API endpoints
  4) Endpoint follow-up strategy
  5) Wordlist generation strategy
  6) Fuzzing strategy
- In the API endpoint section, distinguish directly observed endpoints from inferred endpoints and label confidence.
- In the endpoint follow-up strategy section, for each important endpoint or endpoint family, explain the next investigation steps, what methods/parameters/content-types to test, and what evidence supports the recommendation.
- In the wordlist generation strategy section, include a practical seed wordlist derived from the observed routes/content and also describe an algorithm for expanding it (for example path-segment extraction, query-key extraction, product/domain terminology, plural/singular variants, version prefixes, auth/admin/user/resource nouns).
- In the fuzzing strategy section, describe how to fuzz paths, query parameters, parameter values, headers, methods, content-types, identifiers, pagination, filtering, sorting, versioning, and auth-related variations.
- Prefer concise, operationally useful guidance over generic advice.

Sitemap JSON (possibly truncated):
{{SITEMAP_JSON}}

Endpoint schema JSON (possibly truncated):
{{ENDPOINT_SCHEMA_JSON}}

Unique URLs (sampled {{UNIQUE_URLS_INCLUDED_COUNT}} of {{UNIQUE_URLS_TOTAL_COUNT}}):
{{UNIQUE_URLS_JSON}}

Likely API endpoints (sampled {{API_ENDPOINTS_INCLUDED_COUNT}} of {{API_ENDPOINTS_TOTAL_COUNT}}):
{{API_ENDPOINTS_JSON}}

AI-guided probe results (sampled {{PROBE_RESULTS_INCLUDED_COUNT}} of {{PROBE_RESULTS_TOTAL_COUNT}}):
{{PROBE_RESULTS_JSON}}
additional_urls_prompt:
You are analyzing a website crawl and suggesting additional URLs to probe.
Rules:
- Only suggest URLs on {{ROOT_DOMAIN}} or its subdomains.
- Suggest realistic URLs likely to exist but not already listed.
- Use the endpoint schema as primary source of route/query coverage.
- If schema placeholders appear, materialize with defaults (int=1, string=test, bool=true, uuid=00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000).
- Return STRICT JSON only in this format:
{"additional_urls": ["https://...", "https://..."]}

Endpoint schema JSON (possibly truncated for model context):
{{ENDPOINT_SCHEMA_JSON}}

Existing crawl sitemap JSON:
{{SITEMAP_JSON}}
site_analysis_prompt:
You are a web reconnaissance analyst.
Given a website crawl + model-suggested URLs, produce a comprehensive HTML report. Return ONLY the full HTML document.
The report must include these sections:
1) High-level site analysis
2) Cleaned sitemap grouped by section
3) Likely API endpoints
4) Endpoint follow-up strategy
5) Wordlist generation strategy
6) Fuzzing strategy
7) Additional crawl/test priorities
8) Risks/unknowns

Requirements for the endpoint-focused sections:
- In the API endpoint section, group related endpoints into families when that is clearer, distinguish directly observed endpoints from inferred endpoints, and label confidence.
- In the endpoint follow-up strategy section, for each important endpoint or endpoint family, explain how to approach it next: methods to try, parameter hypotheses, header/content-type hypotheses, auth assumptions, sequencing, and what a useful success/failure signal would look like.
- In the wordlist generation strategy section, provide both:
  - a concrete starter wordlist derived from the crawl data, and
  - an algorithm for generating more candidates from route segments, query keys, page terminology, product nouns, auth/account terms, CRUD verbs, version markers, and singular/plural/resource variants.
- In the fuzzing strategy section, give a practical plan for fuzzing paths, IDs, slugs, query keys, query values, headers, methods, content-types, pagination/filter/sort controls, locale/version toggles, and auth-related behavior.
- Prefer explicit, actionable output tied to the provided data.

Constraints:
- Focus only on {{ROOT_DOMAIN}} and its subdomains.
- If inferring endpoints, mark them as inferred unless directly observed.
- Inputs may be truncated for model context; acknowledge any limits in analysis.

Original discovered URLs (sampled {{ORIGINAL_URLS_INCLUDED_COUNT}} of {{ORIGINAL_URLS_TOTAL_COUNT}}):
{{ORIGINAL_URLS_JSON}}

AI-suggested additional URLs (sampled {{SUGGESTED_URLS_INCLUDED_COUNT}} of {{SUGGESTED_URLS_TOTAL_COUNT}}):
{{SUGGESTED_URLS_JSON}}

Crawl sitemap JSON (possibly truncated):
{{SITEMAP_JSON}}

Endpoint schema JSON (possibly truncated):
{{ENDPOINT_SCHEMA_JSON}}

AI-guided probe results (sampled {{PROBE_RESULTS_INCLUDED_COUNT}} of {{PROBE_RESULTS_TOTAL_COUNT}}):
{{PROBE_RESULTS_JSON}}
probe_requests_prompt:
You are planning the next HTTP requests for web reconnaissance.
You will be given a canonical endpoint schema (host + path + query params with data-type defaults).
Return only safe requests and stay in-domain.
Rules:
- Domain scope is {{ROOT_DOMAIN}} and subdomains only.
- Use only GET or HEAD methods.
- Return at most {{MAX_REQUESTS}} requests.
- Return at most {{PER_HOST_MAX_REQUESTS}} requests per host.
- If placeholders are present, materialize with defaults: int=1, string=test, bool=true, uuid=00000000-0000-4000-8000-000000000000.
- Return STRICT JSON only in this shape:
{"requests":[{"url":"https://...","method":"GET","reason":"..."},{"url":"https://...","method":"HEAD","reason":"..."}]}

Endpoint schema JSON (possibly truncated):
{{ENDPOINT_SCHEMA_JSON}}
